summary.intent: Resume and complete tab persistence in the Gossamer app, where tabs survive page reloads by saving/loading from localStorage.
summary.outcome: Tab persistence was wired up in TabsContext.tsx; earlier in the session, a spawn tab type (replacing launcher) and SpawnSessionScreen were built to auto-navigate to newly spawned sessions.

repo: Tab types include 'spawn' (formerly 'launcher'), with a spawnedAt timestamp used to match new sessions within a 5s window
SessionDetail supports a hideTerminal flag for embedding it inside SpawnSessionScreen
server.ts broadcast poll interval is 500ms; sessions visible only via LogEvent rows are now included in the session list
TabsContext has STORAGE_KEY and loadPersistedState() for localStorage-based tab persistence

code.finding: loadPersistedState() and STORAGE_KEY were added but not wired up initially — needed to be called on init and saved on change
Polls for new session by matching startedAt >= spawnedAt - 5s, then auto-navigates to SessionDetail
Live polling at 500ms; accepts hideTerminal prop for use inside SpawnSessionScreen
Spawning a session opens a 'spawn' tab rather than directly calling the server spawnSession API

workflow: Session resumed mid-task by reading key files and diffing uncommitted changes to reconstruct in-progress state
Incomplete wiring (functions defined but not called) was identified via code review before continuing

friction: Tab persistence code (loadPersistedState, STORAGE_KEY) was written in a prior session but never connected — required auditing what was missing before making progress
open_items: Verify tab persistence handles spawn tab type correctly on reload (spawnedAt timestamp, in-flight spawn state)
SessionRow 'resume' button was removed — confirm this is intentional and no replacement UX is needed
LauncherScreen.tsx was modified — unclear from transcript what changed there
